protocol: full-set blind heuristic-formula, two label-free passes with repeated-candidate consistency audit

isolation_confirmation.statement: Only the two authorized inputs were read. Scores use deterministic arithmetic on visible specifications; no training, simulation, reconstruction, labels, or empirical candidate performance.

mlp.assumptions: D hidden affine layers of constant width W; each true LayerNorm has trainable scale and bias (2W); scalar output; residual paths are parameter-free.

transformer_lm.assumptions: Untied token embedding and LM head; learned positional embedding; Q,K,V,O weights/biases; two affine LayerNorms per block; two-layer biased FFN; final affine LayerNorm.

bigram_lm.rationale: A first-order bigram process needs modest capacity and rapid short-budget optimization; excessive scale is a trainability risk.

multivariate_regression.rationale: The visible 4D target includes additive nonlinearities and multiplicative interactions, favoring moderate depth/width but penalizing very large short-budget models.

univariate_regression.rationale: The target is a noiseless sinusoid plus a linear term; smooth activations and fast optimization matter more than extreme capacity in 64 steps.

anomalies_found: Initial bigram high-adaptive-LR penalties were too mild relative to the 80-step instability risk.
Initial multivariate depth-4 and residual terms underrepresented the visible multiplicative target while large width was partly double rewarded.
Initial univariate scoring underweighted smooth GELU/SiLU activations and was too forgiving of 0.0001 learning rates in only 64 steps.
Scale risk and capacity were partially double-counted; the revision retains a benefit for useful capacity but makes only the excess-scale term more negative.
consistency_audit.dominance_check: No single term is allowed to decide all families: optimizer/LR can be offset by scale, depth, residual, activation, and regularization terms. Step contribution is zero wherever the budget is invariant.
proposed_changes: Strengthen high-LR and excess-scale risk for bigram.
Increase depth-4/residual value and excess-scale penalty for multivariate.
Increase smooth-activation value, reduce deep-model preference, strengthen excess-scale and 0.0001-LR penalties for univariate.

formula_revisions.reason: 80 updates make the highest adaptive LR disproportionately unstable.
Audit separated useful capacity from parameters unlikely to train in 80 steps.
The explicit product terms justify moderate compositional depth.
160 steps remain too few for such a conservative adaptive rate.
Width should not be rewarded twice when the fixed budget limits parameter coverage.
GELU/SiLU better match a smooth sinusoidal target than piecewise-linear-only stacks.
64 steps and a one-dimensional smooth target weaken the case for depth.
Consistency audit found systematic underfitting risk at the smallest adaptive rate.
Very wide models are unlikely to exploit their capacity in 64 updates.

bigram_lm.changes: Adam/AdamW@0.01=-1.8 plus -0.8 instability interaction; excess-scale risk strengthened to -0.3/-0.6/-0.9.

multivariate_regression.changes: D4=1.4, D5=1.2, D6=0.8; deep residual=0.5; smooth fraction coefficient=0.6; Adam/AdamW@0.0001=-0.6; scale risk=-0.3/-0.7/-1.2.

univariate_regression.changes: Smooth fraction coefficient=0.8; D5=0.7,D6=0.2; Adam/AdamW@0.0001=-1.0; scale risk=-0.3/-0.7/-1.2.

final_reflection.main_drivers: Non-monotonic optimizer-specific LR fit dominated when differences were large; capacity, depth, smooth activation, residuals, weight decay, and excess-scale risk resolved closer comparisons.
final_reflection.fixed_budget_handling: Steps and batch size were recorded for each initial choice, but their direct term is zero because they are invariant within every question; they enter through family-wide short-budget interactions.
uncertainties: Exact initialization and implementation details, including embedding tying and residual projection behavior, are sanitized or unspecified.
A heuristic cannot know whether a nominally aggressive LR converges unusually well in a particular deterministic run.
Parameter count is a trainability proxy, not a direct generalization metric; normalization can change effective optimization substantially.
final_reflection.label_free_confirmation: All revisions came from visible target complexity, repeated-candidate consistency, and double-counting checks, never labels or empirical feedback.
